title: Week 3. Listening in the AI Age (Visual Version)
text:
How We Really Listen
Listening is not passive.
It is selective and limited.


Selectivity (Concept)
We cannot process everything.
We choose what to hear.


Selectivity (Visual)
Imagine: - Many voices -> one heard - Many messages -> few remembered
Diagram idea:
[Many inputs] -> [Filter] -> [Selected info]


Iyengar Experiment
24 options vs 6 options
Result:
Fewer choices -> more decisions


Iyengar (Visual)
Diagram:
24 choices -> overload -> no decision
6 choices -> manageable -> decision


Information Overload
Too much information -> less listening
Example:
Endless news, SNS feeds


Bias in Listening
We prefer familiar ideas.
This is:
Confirmation bias


Echo Chamber (Visual)
Diagram:
User -> similar content -> reinforced belief -> repeat
Cycle continues.


Reference Point
We judge relatively.
Example:
Big first -> small feels smaller


Contrast Effect (Visual)
Diagram:
Big stimulus -> Medium stimulus -> perceived smaller


Kahneman: Prospect Theory
We think in gain vs loss, not absolute value.


Prospect Theory (Visual)
Diagram:
Reference point
Gain (+)
Loss (-, stronger impact)


Peak-End Rule
We remember: - Peak moment - Ending


Peak-End (Visual)
Timeline:
Start -> Peak -> End
Memory focuses on:
Peak + End


Human Listening Limits

Selective
Biased
Context-dependent



Enter AI
AI processes large data.


AI vs Human (Visual)
Human:
Limited -> selective
AI:
Large input -> broad processing


Externalized Listening
Listening moves outside the brain.
Example:
Automatic news summary


System vs Tool
Tool:
Ask -> answer
System:
Collect -> filter -> analyze


Automated Listening System (Visual)
Data -> Collection -> Filtering -> Analysis -> Insight


Example: News Analysis
Thousands of articles -> filtered -> summarized


Example: Interview Analysis
Many voices -> transcription -> categorization -> pattern detection


Research to Everyday
Before:
Experts only
Now:
Everyone can use


Invisible Process
System works automatically.
User sees only the result.


New Inequality
Old:
Ability
New:
System


Final Question
What system do you listen with?
